title: Git — Branching & Merging
philosophy: Wing Chun teaches us to deflect and redirect force rather than opposing it. A Git branch allows you to divert the flow of time, safely experimenting with alternate 'genealogical' lineages of code before merging them back to the main trunk.
economics: In macro-economics, this is Research & Development (R&D). You isolate risk in a separate sandbox (branch) so experimental failures do not crash the primary market (main branch).
techconcept: Branches let you isolate work-in-progress. The command `git checkout -b <name>` both creates a new branch and switches to it.
lesson: Using branches, you can try bold ideas without endangering main. When the work is ready, you merge the branch back, preserving the experimental history. This encourages exploration while protecting stability.
challenge: What command would you use to create and switch to a new branch named 'feature'?
hint: You combine 'checkout' with '-b' and the branch name.
